statements.status_notes: CVE-2026-39821 affects golang.org/x/net before v0.55.0 (x/net/idna); the scanner flagged kubectl 1.36.2, which shipped golang.org/x/net v0.49.0. Fixed upstream: Kubernetes now vendors golang.org/x/net >= v0.55.0 (currently v0.55.1-0.20260602153038-42abb857022c), so the vulnerable version is no longer present. Related dependency bumps: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/138561, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/139420. [ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/140092]

CVE-2025-68121 (Go stdlib crypto/tls — unexpected TLS session resumption) affects kubectl binaries built with an unpatched Go. Fixed in Go 1.24.13 / 1.25.7 / 1.26.0-rc.3; a maintainer confirmed the Go bump was already merged into supported Kubernetes branches and pending the next release when these issues closed. Reported independently in #137195, #137228, and #140091. [sources: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/137195, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/137228, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/140091]

CVE-2026-33811 (Go stdlib) affects kubectl binaries for 1.33/1.34/1.35 built with Go 1.25.9. Fixed in Go 1.26.3+; the Go 1.26.4 bump landed on master (#139479) and release-1.36 (#138871). Backports to the supported 1.34/1.35 branches follow the monthly patch cadence (a maintainer pointed to the security/patch process rather than confirming per-branch in-issue). Reported in #139510 and #140091. [sources: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/139510, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/140091]

CVE-2026-33814 (Go stdlib) affects kubectl binaries for 1.33/1.34/1.35 built with Go 1.25.9. Fixed in Go 1.26.3+; the Go 1.26.4 bump landed on master (#139479) and release-1.36 (#138871). Backports to the supported 1.34/1.35 branches follow the monthly patch cadence (a maintainer pointed to the security/patch process rather than confirming per-branch in-issue). Reported in #139510 and #140091. [sources: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/139510, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/140091]

CVE-2026-39836 (Go stdlib net — panic on NUL byte in Dial/LookupPort on Windows) affects kubectl binaries for 1.33/1.34/1.35 built with Go 1.25.9. Fixed in Go 1.26.3+; the Go 1.26.4 bump landed on master (#139479) and release-1.36 (#138871). Backports to the supported 1.34/1.35 branches follow the monthly patch cadence. Reported in #139510 and #140091. [sources: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/139510, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/140091]

Go stdlib CVEs in kubectl binaries for 1.33/1.34/1.35. Go 1.26.4 merged to master (#139479) and release-1.36 (#138871). Supported-branch backports were requested; maintainer pointed to the security/patch process rather than confirming per-branch backport in-issue. Fixed on master/1.36; supported-branch Go bumps handled via monthly patch cadence. [ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/139510]

Go stdlib CVEs in kubectl binaries for 1.33/1.34/1.35. Go 1.26.4 merged to master (#139479) and release-1.36 (#138871). Fixed on master/1.36; supported-branch Go bumps handled via monthly patch cadence. [ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/139510]

Go stdlib CVEs in kubectl binaries for 1.33/1.34/1.35. Go 1.26.4 merged to master (#139479) and release-1.36 (#138871). Supported-branch backports were requested; maintainer pointed to the security/patch process rather than confirming per-branch backport in-issue. Fixed on master/1.36; supported-branch Go bumps handled via monthly patch cadence. [ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/139510]

A maintainer determined Kubernetes has no in-tree gRPC servers using path-based authorization interceptors (google.golang.org/grpc/authz RBAC or custom interceptors relying on info.FullMethod / grpc.Method(ctx)) with 'deny' rules plus a default 'allow' fallback — the two conditions GHSA-p77j-4mvh-x3m3 requires. The vulnerable code path is not present. Reporter provided only a scanner hit. [ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/139221]

The vulnerable code (go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/resource host_id_bsd.go, which runs 'kenv' without an absolute path) is gated behind DragonFly/FreeBSD/NetBSD/OpenBSD/Solaris build tags. Kubernetes ships only linux/windows/darwin binaries, so the vulnerable BSD-specific code is not compiled into shipped artifacts. A maintainer confirmed Kubernetes does not ship binaries or images for any of the affected platforms, so it is not affected. [ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/138329]

CVE-2026-25679 (Go stdlib) affects kubectl binaries built with Go 1.25.7. Fixed in Go 1.25.8 / 1.26.1; a maintainer confirmed Go was already updated across all supported Kubernetes branches with patch releases in progress at close. Reported in #137853 and #138040 (the latter mistyped the ID as 'CVE-2026-2567'). [sources: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/137853, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/138040]

Go stdlib CVEs in kubectl v1.33.5. A maintainer confirmed Go was already updated to 1.24.9 on release-1.33 but not yet released; fix merged, release pending at close. [ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/135083]
